2605.13723.title: Humanwashing -- It Should Leave You Feeling Dirty
2605.13723.paper_summary: The phrase 'human in the loop' is increasingly used to imply a sense of safety in relation to AI decision systems. It shouldn't. There are contexts where it can be applied appropriately, but these are not in the deployed decision systems we see dominating today. Human oversight of AI decision processes is one of the most popular proposals for addressing concerns, especially about bias, discrimination, misinformation, manipulation, accountability, and transparency. But there is insufficient examination of what human oversight actually means. The question raised in this paper is whether using the metaphor of a loop does anything to assist understanding of what is required and what is achieved in a particular decision context. Indiscriminate use of the loop metaphor obscures both processes and outcomes. It enables 'humanwashing', an activity analogous to 'greenwashing', where writers and commentators use language primarily aimed at putting systems in the best possible light.
2605.13723.paper_summary_zh: 「人類在循環中」這個詞語越來越多地被用來暗示與人工智慧決策系統相關的安全感。它不應該如此。雖然在某些情境下可以適當應用，但這些情境並不在我們今天所見的主導決策系統中。對人工智慧決策過程的人類監督是解決偏見、歧視、錯誤資訊、操控、問責和透明度等問題的最受歡迎提議之一。但對於人類監督實際上意味著什麼的檢視仍然不足。本文提出的問題是，使用循環的隱喻是否有助於理解在特定決策情境中所需的和所達成的內容。隨意使用循環隱喻模糊了過程和結果。它使得「人類洗白」成為可能，這一活動類似於「綠色洗白」，作家和評論者使用的語言主要旨在將系統呈現為最佳狀態。
2605.13723.author: Ben Wilson et.al.
2605.13723.authors: Ben Wilson, Matimba Swana, Peter Winter, Matt Roach
